title: senna circinnata
intro: Senna circinnata, also known as winter cassia, is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its laxative properties, as it contains anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate the bowel movements and relieve constipation. The medicinal properties of Senna circinnata include treating digestive issues, such as constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as providing relief from fever and inflammation. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance its therapeutic effects.
